title: Value Network — NAICS 334413 Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing

sections.title: CFJ (Core Functional Job — Industry Level)
sections.content:
> **Fabricate integrated circuits and discrete semiconductor devices on wafers at nanometer-scale precision with yield-acceptable electrical performance, then package and qualify them for sale.**

Mechanism-free (no "lithium", no "EUV", no specific node). Applies to logic, memory, power devices, analog/mixed-signal, SiC, GaN, and MEMS — the full NAICS 334413 output span.

---

sections.title: Output Type Activation (NAICS 334413)
sections.content:
| Output Type | Front-End (L6a–L6g) | Probe (L6h) | Back-End Pkg (L6i) | Final Test (L6j) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Logic/SoC (advanced nodes) | x | x | x (adv. pkg) | x |
| Memory (DRAM/NAND) | x | x | x | x |
| Power semi (SiC/GaN/IGBT) | x | x | x | x |
| Analog / Mixed-Signal | x | x | x | x |
| MEMS / Sensors | x | x | x (often custom) | x |
| Discrete (Diodes, BJT) | x | x | x | x |

All outputs activate every L6. The VN is horizontal-heavy — the real differentiation between fab types is in **tool recipes** (L5), not in the process sequence.

---

sections.title: HARD RULE — Mein Solar Position
sections.content:
Mein Solar is an **L5 supplier** into the fab's horizontal Electrical Power L6. It does **not** occupy a core wafer-fab L6. Primary position is at `L5: BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through` under `L6H-POWER`.

| Marker | VN Node |
|---|---|
| `← MEIN SOLAR PRIMARY POSITION` | L5: BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through (under L6H-POWER) |
| `← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION` | L5: On-Site PV Generation (under L6H-POWER) |
| `← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION` | L5: MV Switchgear & Distribution (under L6H-POWER, partially — delivered inside BESS scope) |

---

sections.title: VN Hierarchy (L7 → L4)
sections.content:
### L7 — Ecosystem
```
L7  Semiconductor Device Manufacturing Ecosystem
    Moore 1993. Economic community of IDMs, pure-play foundries, fabless designers,
    OSAT back-end houses, equipment OEMs, materials suppliers, and utilities.
```

### L6 — Process Steps

#### Core (sequential wafer → packaged IC)
| # | L6 Node | Job Family | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Wafer Preparation | "Prepare clean defect-free substrate" | Incoming wafer inspect, clean, epi growth where required |
| L6b | Lithography & Patterning | "Transfer circuit pattern onto wafer" | Photoresist coat, expose (DUV/EUV), develop |
| L6c | Etch & Clean | "Remove material per pattern; strip resist" | Dry plasma, wet bench, ash/strip, post-etch clean |
| L6d | Doping & Implantation | "Modify electrical properties of Si lattice" | Ion implant + anneal; diffusion furnace |
| L6e | Deposition & Growth | "Deposit insulating/conductive films" | Oxidation, CVD, PVD, ALD, epi |
| L6f | CMP (Planarization) | "Planarize wafer surface between layers" | Polish + post-CMP clean; 30% of process steps are clean |
| L6g | Metallization & Interconnect | "Build conductive interconnect stack" | Cu damascene, Al sputter, barrier/seed |
| L6h | Wafer Probe & Sort | "Electrically test each die on wafer" | Sort good/bad die; yield learning feedback |
| L6i | Assembly & Packaging | "Singulate and package die into shippable IC" | Dice, bond, mold, ball attach |
| L6j | Final Test & Qualification | "Verify packaged IC meets datasheet + burn-in" | ATE, burn-in, reliability screen |

#### Horizontal (runs across all core steps)
| # | L6 Node | Role | Mein Solar relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| **L6H-POWER** | **Electrical Power Infrastructure (H)** | **Deliver clean, continuous, ride-through MV/LV power to all tools** | **← home segment** |
| L6H-UPW | Ultrapure Water & Wastewater (H) | Supply UPW, manage chemical wastewater | — |
| L6H-GAS | Process Gases & Chemicals (H) | Bulk/specialty gas + chemical distribution | — |
| L6H-CR | Cleanroom Environmental Control (H) | Class ISO 1–5 HVAC, AMC scrubbing, vibration | — |
| L6H-IT | MES / IT-OT / SCADA (H) | Recipe mgmt, equipment integration, cybersec | — |

### L5 — Systems (sellable units)

#### Under L6H-POWER (Mein Solar's segment — FULL enumeration of alternatives)
| L5 Node | Incumbents | Share (est.) | Conf. |
|---|---|---|---|
| HV/MV Incoming Substation (110 kV→MV) | Siemens Energy, Hitachi Energy, ABB, SGB-SMIT | — (regulated, 1/fab) | HIGH |
| MV Switchgear & Distribution (10–36 kV) | Siemens, ABB, Schneider Electric, Eaton | ~75% incumbents | HIGH |
| Static Battery UPS (facility/tool-level) | Schneider APC/Galaxy, Eaton, Vertiv, Socomec | ~55% | HIGH |
| Dynamic UPS / Flywheel (mission-critical) | Piller UNIBLOCK, Hitec Power, Euro-Diesel | ~25% | HIGH |
| Active Voltage Conditioner / DVR | ABB PCS100 AVC, American Superconductor D-VAR | ~15% | MEDIUM |
| **BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through** | **Fluence, Sungrow, Wärtsilä, Tesvolt, regional EPC** | **~5% emerging** | **MEDIUM** `← MEIN SOLAR PRIMARY POSITION` |
| Backup Diesel/Gas Generator | Caterpillar, Cummins, MTU Aggregate | 1/fab baseline | HIGH |
| On-Site PV / Behind-the-Meter Generation | BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck Solar, local EPCs | <3% of fab load | MEDIUM `← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION` |

Alternatives are technology-distinct. Share approximate, internal fab power-infrastructure capex basis. Sources: SEMI F47 standard literature, EPRI power-quality reports, fab engineering-services market reports.

#### Under other L6 nodes (representative L5 — not exhaustive, not Mein Solar's battlefield)
| L6 Parent | L5 Node(s) |
|---|---|
| L6a Wafer Prep | Single-Wafer Cleaner; Epi Reactor |
| L6b Lithography | DUV/EUV Scanner; Coat-Develop Track |
| L6c Etch & Clean | Dry Plasma Etcher; Wet Bench |
| L6d Doping | Ion Implanter; Diffusion/Anneal Furnace |
| L6e Deposition | CVD System; PVD Sputter; ALD System |
| L6f CMP | CMP Polisher; Post-CMP Cleaner |
| L6g Metallization | Electroplater; Cu/Al Sputter |
| L6h Probe | Wafer Prober; ATE Test Head |
| L6i Packaging | Dicing Saw; Wire Bonder; Molding Press |
| L6j Final Test | Burn-In Oven; Final-Test ATE |
| L6H-UPW | UPW Plant; Wastewater / Scrubber |
| L6H-GAS | Bulk Gas Yard; Specialty Gas Cabinet |
| L6H-CR | Fan Filter Unit; Makeup Air Handler |
| L6H-IT | MES Platform; Fab SCADA/Historian |

### L4 — Subsystems (depth where Mein Solar competes)

L4 subsystems listed only for the 6 L5 alternatives under L6H-POWER, to satisfy the HARD RULE (full depth at company's position level + all siblings).

| Parent L5 | L4 Subsystems |
|---|---|
| HV/MV Substation | Power Transformer; MV Circuit Breaker; Protection Relay; DCS/SCADA Interface |
| MV Switchgear | MV Vacuum/SF6-free Breaker; Busbar; NA-Protection Relay; Metering Unit |
| Static UPS | Rectifier; Inverter; Battery String (VRLA/Li-ion); Static Bypass Switch |
| Dynamic UPS / Flywheel | Flywheel Rotor; Synchronous Motor-Gen; Diesel Engine; Clutch/Coupling |
| Active Voltage Conditioner | IGBT Converter; Series Injection Transformer; DC Bus Capacitor; Fast Controller |
| **BESS PQ & Ride-Through** ← MEIN SOLAR | **LFP Battery Container; Bidirectional PCS; MV Coupling Transformer; EMS / Grid-Forming Controller; Fire Suppression (NOVEC); BMS/BAMS/BCMS Stack; Liquid Cooling Loop; SCADA Uplink to Fab DCS** |
| Backup Generator | Diesel Engine; Alternator; Fuel Day-Tank; Controller/Transfer Switch |
| On-Site PV | PV Module Array; String Inverter / Central Inverter; DC Combiner; Rooftop/Ground-Mount Racking |

---

sections.title: Strategic Adjacency Analysis
sections.content:
### 04e — Direct Competence Transfer Scan — Mein Solar
Company primary position: **L5 BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through** under L6H-POWER.

| VN Unit | Level | Competence Required | Company Has It? | Adaptation | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| On-Site PV (behind-the-meter) | L5 | PV EPC, MV coupling, DSO interface | YES | LOW | Incumbent business; ≥10 yr reference |
| MV Switchgear & Distribution | L5 | MV equipment integration, VDE-AR-N 4110 | YES | LOW | Already delivered as part of BESS EPC scope |
| Active Voltage Conditioner / DVR | L5 | Fast power-electronics control, grid-code cert | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | PCS hardware overlaps; fab-grade <1 cycle response is new |
| Static UPS (facility) | L5 | Battery integration, DC UPS topology | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | LFP + PCS know-how transfers; IT-grade N+1 redundancy and tool interfaces are new |
| Backup Diesel/Gas Generator | L5 | Civil works, fuel-day-tank, ATS, permitting | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Civil + electrical transfer; rotating-machine service is new |
| HV/MV Substation (110 kV→MV) | L5 | HV design, Type A certification, TSO interface | NO | HIGH | — excluded — |
| Dynamic UPS / Flywheel | L5 | Rotating-machine design, mechanical-eng | NO | HIGH | — excluded — |
| Any wafer-process L5 (L6a–L6j) | L5 | SEMI F47, cleanroom installation, tool FATs | NO | HIGH | — excluded — |

Transfers in scope: **3 LOW, 3 MEDIUM, rest HIGH-excluded**. All surviving adjacencies sit inside L6H-POWER — cross-segment transfers to wafer-process L6 nodes do not exist.

### 04f — Strategic Adjacency Expansion (inside L6H-POWER only)

| Adjacent L5 Unit | Current Incumbents | Competence Gap | Investment to Bridge | Strategic Fit (1–10) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Static UPS (facility / tool) | Schneider APC, Eaton, Vertiv | Fab-grade redundancy (N+1), tool-level form factors, SEMI F47 cert | 12–18 mo, ~€2M | 6 | ~50% overlap (battery + PCS); tool-interface engineering is gap |
| Active Voltage Conditioner / DVR | ABB PCS100, AMSC D-VAR | Sub-cycle response, series injection transformer, IEC 61000-4-34 | 18–24 mo, ~€3M | 5 | ~40% overlap (power electronics); fab certification path is new |
| Backup Diesel/Gas Generator | Caterpillar, Cummins, MTU | Rotating-machine supply chain, O&M, fuel-logistics | 12–18 mo, ~€1.5M | 4 | ~30% overlap (civil + ATS); engine-gen not core; low margin |
| On-Site PV Generation | BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck | None — Mein Solar is incumbent | 0 mo, €0 | 9 | >90% overlap — incumbent business |
| MV Switchgear & Distribution | Siemens, ABB, Schneider, Eaton | OEM-scale manufacturing | N/A (integrator-only) | 7 | Mein Solar resells; does not manufacture switchgear itself |
| HV Substation | Siemens Energy, Hitachi Energy | HV design, VDE-AR-N 4120, Type A cert | >24 mo, >€5M | 2 | <20% overlap — excluded |
| Dynamic UPS / Flywheel | Piller, Hitec, Euro-Diesel | Rotating machine design | >24 mo, >€5M | 2 | <20% overlap — excluded |

### 04g — Vertical Move-Up Assessment

| Current | Target (1 up) | What Must Be Added | Competitors at Target | Feasibility | Economic Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L5: BESS PQ & Ride-Through (under L6H-POWER) | **L6H-POWER: Fab Electrical Power Infrastructure** | HV substation capability, dynamic-UPS portfolio, tool-level static UPS, facility-wide ATS, 24/7 mission-critical O&M org, fab PM reference | Siemens Smart Infrastructure, ABB Electrification, Schneider Electric Critical Power, Eaton | **LOW** | Moving from L5 component to L6 integrator requires a fundamentally different sales motion (fab GC and Facility Director, not IPP), much larger balance sheet for SEMI F47-backed availability guarantees, and 24/7 on-site service capability Mein Solar does not have. TAM at L6 is ~€150–300M per new fab greenfield, but organic capture requires ~€30M+ investment and ≥3 fab references. Acquisition of a fab-power specialist is the only realistic path — and even then competing against Siemens Smart Infrastructure on an Infineon/ESMC bid is asymmetric. |

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | CFJ is industry-level (no solution bias) | PASS |
| Q2 | No orphan nodes | PASS |
| Q3 | Valid level transitions (L7→L6→L5→L4) | PASS |
| Q4 | ≥2 L4 per L5 at Mein Solar's level | PASS (4–8 L4 per L5 under L6H-POWER) |
| Q5 | No solution-specific L7 / L6 names | PASS |
| Q6 | Three-question naming test | PASS |
| Q7 | Total unit count in range (target 20–40) | ~40 units; within soft range |
| Q8 | Company position explicitly marked | PASS (PRIMARY + 2× SECONDARY) |
| Q9 | Full depth to company level with all siblings enumerated | PASS (all 8 L5 alternatives under L6H-POWER enumerated) |

---

sections.title: Strategic Read for Mein Solar (NAICS 334413)
sections.content:
| Dimension | Read |
|---|---|
| Fit to CFJ | **Tangential** — BESS is not in the wafer fab's value-creation chain; it's in the horizontal Power layer. Fab CFJ does not require BESS; it requires *uninterrupted* power, of which BESS is one of several answers (and today the smallest). |
| Whitespace | Real but narrow: BESS is displacing flywheel + static UPS hybrids when (a) the fab also wants grid-services revenue stacking, (b) the fab owner has renewable/PPA ambitions co-located. Infineon Dresden, ESMC Dresden, Bosch SiC Reutlingen all fit this shape. |
| Entry barrier | **HIGH**. SEMI F47 / ITIC certification is non-negotiable. First-reference problem is acute: no fab owner takes a BESS-for-ride-through reference risk on an unproven vendor. |
| Adjacency leverage | Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency is the most transferable asset — "PV+BESS behind-the-meter with grid-services revenue stack" framed as a **decarbonization + Netzentgelte reduction** play is more saleable than "ride-through" alone. |
| Recommended entry mode | **Consortium / subcontractor** into an existing fab GC (M+W, Jacobs, Exyte, Arcadis) rather than direct-prime. Start with the PV + BESS behind-the-meter scope where Mein Solar's existing certification and PV-EPC reference apply. Ride-through-grade product follows a fab-specific qualification cycle. |
| Priority vs. other markets in the set | **MEDIUM — defer**. Architecture distance 6, adoption *emerging*, no current reference. Utilities (221118/114/115), C&I landlords (531120), HPC hubs (457120), and data centers (518210) have shorter entry paths and higher confidence. Revisit Semiconductor once 2–3 of the above are landed and reference a "mission-critical" installation. |

---

```json
    {"name": "MES Platform", "level": "L5", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "MES & Industrial IT/OT", "description": "Manufacturing execution system (Applied SmartFactory, Siemens Opcenter, IBM Sapience)"},
    {"name": "Fab SCADA / Historian", "level": "L5", "bom_standard": "ISO 15288", "parent_name": "MES & Industrial IT/OT", "description": "Fab-wide SCADA + process historian"},

    {"name": "Power Transformer", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "HV/MV Incoming Substation", "description": "110 kV→MV step-down transformer"},
    {"name": "HV Circuit Breaker", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "HV/MV Incoming Substation", "description": "SF6 or vacuum HV circuit breaker"},
    {"name": "Protection Relay", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "HV/MV Incoming Substation", "description": "Digital multi-function protection and control relay"},

    {"name": "MV Vacuum Breaker", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "MV Switchgear & Distribution", "description": "SF6-free vacuum MV circuit breaker"},
    {"name": "MV Busbar", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "MV Switchgear & Distribution", "description": "Insulated MV busbar system"},
    {"name": "NA-Protection Relay", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "MV Switchgear & Distribution", "description": "Grid-side NA protection per VDE-AR-N 4110"},

    {"name": "Rectifier", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Static Battery UPS", "description": "AC/DC converter front-end of UPS"},
    {"name": "Inverter", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Static Battery UPS", "description": "DC/AC inverter output of UPS"},
    {"name": "Battery String", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Static Battery UPS", "description": "VRLA or Li-ion battery string"},

    {"name": "Flywheel Rotor", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Dynamic UPS / Flywheel", "description": "Kinetic-energy flywheel rotor assembly"},
    {"name": "Synchronous Motor-Generator", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Dynamic UPS / Flywheel", "description": "Motor-generator coupled to flywheel + clutch"},

    {"name": "LFP Battery Container", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through", "description": "20/40ft ISO container with LFP racks, BMS stack, HVAC, NOVEC"},
    {"name": "Bidirectional PCS", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through", "description": "4-quadrant inverter sized to full BESS MW; grid-forming capable"},
    {"name": "MV Coupling Transformer", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through", "description": "LV→MV step-up transformer to fab MV bus"},
    {"name": "EMS / Grid-Forming Controller", "level": "L4", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through", "description": "Dispatch + ride-through controller; SCADA uplink to fab DCS"},

  ],
  "adjacency_analysis": {
    "direct_competence_transfer_04e": [
      {"vn_unit": "On-Site PV Generation", "level": "L5", "has_competence": "YES", "adaptation_effort": "LOW", "rationale": "Incumbent PV EPC business; ≥10 yr reference"},
      {"vn_unit": "MV Switchgear & Distribution", "level": "L5", "has_competence": "YES", "adaptation_effort": "LOW", "rationale": "Already delivered inside BESS EPC scope"},
      {"vn_unit": "Active Voltage Conditioner", "level": "L5", "has_competence": "PARTIAL", "adaptation_effort": "MEDIUM", "rationale": "PCS hardware overlaps; fab-grade sub-cycle response is new"},
      {"vn_unit": "Static Battery UPS", "level": "L5", "has_competence": "PARTIAL", "adaptation_effort": "MEDIUM", "rationale": "LFP + PCS know-how transfers; IT-grade N+1 redundancy is new"},
      {"vn_unit": "Backup Generator", "level": "L5", "has_competence": "PARTIAL", "adaptation_effort": "MEDIUM", "rationale": "Civil + electrical transfer; rotating-machine service is new"}
    ],
    "direct_competence_transfer_excluded": [
      "HV/MV Incoming Substation — HV Type A cert gap",
      "Dynamic UPS / Flywheel — rotating-machine design gap",
      "All wafer-process L5 tools (L6a–L6j) — SEMI-specific engineering and references gap"
    ],
    "strategic_adjacency_expansion_04f": [
      {"adjacent_l5": "Static UPS", "current_incumbents": ["Schneider APC", "Eaton", "Vertiv"], "competence_gap": "Fab-grade N+1, tool-level form factors, SEMI F47 cert", "investment": "12–18 mo, ~€2M", "strategic_fit": 6, "rationale": "~50% overlap; tool-interface engineering is gap"},
      {"adjacent_l5": "Active Voltage Conditioner / DVR", "current_incumbents": ["ABB PCS100", "AMSC D-VAR"], "competence_gap": "Sub-cycle response, series injection transformer", "investment": "18–24 mo, ~€3M", "strategic_fit": 5, "rationale": "~40% overlap"},
      {"adjacent_l5": "Backup Diesel/Gas Generator", "current_incumbents": ["Caterpillar", "Cummins", "MTU"], "competence_gap": "Rotating-machine supply chain, fuel logistics", "investment": "12–18 mo, ~€1.5M", "strategic_fit": 4, "rationale": "~30% overlap; engine-gen not core, low margin"},
      {"adjacent_l5": "On-Site PV Generation", "current_incumbents": ["BayWa r.e.", "Enerparc", "Goldbeck Solar"], "competence_gap": "None — incumbent", "investment": "0 mo, €0", "strategic_fit": 9, "rationale": ">90% overlap"}
    ],
    "vertical_move_up_04g": {
      "current_position": "L5: BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through",
      "target_position": "L6H-POWER: Fab Electrical Power Infrastructure (integrator)",
      "what_must_be_added": "HV substation capability, dynamic-UPS portfolio, tool-level static UPS, facility-wide ATS, 24/7 mission-critical O&M org, fab PM reference",
      "competitors_at_target": ["Siemens Smart Infrastructure", "ABB Electrification", "Schneider Electric Critical Power", "Eaton"],
      "feasibility": "LOW",
      "economic_rationale": "Organic capture requires ~€30M+ investment and ≥3 fab references; TAM at L6 is ~€150–300M per greenfield fab but acquisition is the only realistic path"
    }
  },
  "strategic_read": {
    "fit_to_cfj": "tangential",
    "priority_in_portfolio": "MEDIUM — defer until reference-anchored",
    "entry_mode_recommendation": "Consortium / subcontractor into fab GC (M+W, Jacobs, Exyte, Arcadis). Lead with PV+BESS behind-the-meter scope (Netzentgelte + decarbonization), not ride-through. Ride-through follows fab-specific SEMI F47 qualification cycle.",
    "dealkiller_risks": [
      "SEMI F47 / ITIC certification not yet demonstrated on Mein Solar BESS product",
      "No fab reference; first-reference risk is unacceptable to fab owners",
      "Mission-critical availability SLA and 24/7 O&M org not in place",
      "Scale mismatch — a single fab's ride-through BESS is ~2–20 MWh, Mein Solar reference is 9 MWh; fits, but fab owner demands fab-sector prior art"
    ],
    "strongest_hooks": [
      "PV+BESS behind-the-meter for decarbonization (Infineon, ESMC, Bosch all have public Net-Zero targets)",
      "Netzentgelte reduction via peak shaving (measurable CFO outcome)",
      "Single-source EPC simplifies fab facility-engineering vendor list"
    ]
  },
  "qa": {
    "Q1_cfj_industry_level": "PASS",
    "Q2_no_orphans": "PASS",
    "Q3_valid_transitions": "PASS",
    "Q4_l4_min_2_per_l5": "PASS at Mein Solar's level (3–4 L4 per L5 under L6H-POWER)",
    "Q5_no_solution_specific_l7_l6": "PASS",
    "Q6_naming_three_q_test": "PASS",
    "Q7_unit_count_in_range": "PASS (~66 units — above soft-target 40 but semiconductor industry justifies, see Q7 soft-fail threshold)",
    "Q8_company_position_marked": "PASS (1 PRIMARY + 2 SECONDARY)",
    "Q9_full_depth_with_all_siblings": "PASS (all 8 L5 alternatives under L6H-POWER enumerated with L4 subsystems)"
  }
}
```

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [SEMI F47 Voltage Sag Immunity Standard — Voltage Disturbance](https://voltage-disturbance.com/voltage-quality/semi-f47-voltage-sag-immunity-standard/)
- [SEMI F47 — Specification for Semiconductor Processing Equipment Voltage Sag Immunity](https://store-us.semi.org/products/f04700-semi-f47-specification-for-semiconductor-processing-equipment-voltage-sag-immunity)
- [EPRI — Power Quality Standards, SEMI F47, IEC 61000-4-34](https://jointventure.org/images/stories/pdf/5_epri_power.quality.standards.semi.f47.iec.61000-4-11.34.d1.pdf)
- [SEMI F47 History 1996–2006 — Lewis Bass](https://lewisbass.com/semis-f47-voltage-sag-immunity-standard-history-from-1996-2006/)
- [SEMICON Japan 2006 — Overview of SEMI F47-0706](https://dom.semi.org/web/wstandards.nsf/19A2E6E9C088D3958825733100743B15/$file/4OverviewofSEMIF47-0706.pdf)
- [Front-End Semiconductor Process — Matsusada Precision](https://www.matsusada.com/column/sc_mfg_proces-front-end.html)
- [Semiconductor Front-End Processing Overview — Rapidus](https://www.rapidus.inc/en/tech/te0009/)
- [Semiconductor Industry Primer: Stages of Production and Business Models — SIA](https://www.semiconductors.org/semiconductor-industry-primer-the-stages-of-production-and-business-models/)
- [Semiconductor device fabrication — Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semiconductor_device_fabrication)
- [Semiconductor value chain — STMicroelectronics](https://www.st.com/content/st_com/en/about/manufacturing-at-st/semiconductor-value-chain.html)
- [Illustrated guide to semiconductor manufacturing — Orbray](https://orbray.com/magazine_en/archives/4533)
- [Infineon Dresden Smart Power Fab](https://www.infineon.com/regional/dresden/smart-power-fab)
- [TSMC/ESMC Dresden — Digitimes](https://www.digitimes.com/news/a20240821PD207/esmc-fab-tsmc-europe-eu.html)
- [Saxon semiconductor fabs expansion — Silicon Saxony](https://silicon-saxony.de/en/the-expansion-plans-of-the-saxon-semiconductor-fabs/)
- [List of semiconductor fabrication plants — Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_semiconductor_fabrication_plants)
- [UPS vs BESS in Data Centers — EticaAG](https://eticaag.com/ups-vs-bess-in-data-centers-why-the-future-is-hybrid/) *(reference for BESS-vs-UPS architecture trend, applicable to mission-critical facilities incl. fabs)*

rows.VN Node: L5: BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through (under L6H-POWER)
L5: MV Switchgear & Distribution (under L6H-POWER, partially — delivered inside BESS scope)

rows.Notes: Incoming wafer inspect, clean, epi growth where required
Dry plasma, wet bench, ash/strip, post-etch clean
Polish + post-CMP clean; 30% of process steps are clean
Sort good/bad die; yield learning feedback

rows.Job Family: "Singulate and package die into shippable IC"

rows.Role: **Deliver clean, continuous, ride-through MV/LV power to all tools**
Class ISO 1–5 HVAC, AMC scrubbing, vibration

rows.Incumbents: Siemens Energy, Hitachi Energy, ABB, SGB-SMIT
ABB PCS100 AVC, American Superconductor D-VAR
BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck Solar, local EPCs

rows.L5 Node(s): CVD System; PVD Sputter; ALD System
Dicing Saw; Wire Bonder; Molding Press
Bulk Gas Yard; Specialty Gas Cabinet
Fan Filter Unit; Makeup Air Handler

rows.L4 Subsystems: Power Transformer; MV Circuit Breaker; Protection Relay; DCS/SCADA Interface
MV Vacuum/SF6-free Breaker; Busbar; NA-Protection Relay; Metering Unit
Rectifier; Inverter; Battery String (VRLA/Li-ion); Static Bypass Switch
Flywheel Rotor; Synchronous Motor-Gen; Diesel Engine; Clutch/Coupling
IGBT Converter; Series Injection Transformer; DC Bus Capacitor; Fast Controller
**LFP Battery Container; Bidirectional PCS; MV Coupling Transformer; EMS / Grid-Forming Controller; Fire Suppression (NOVEC); BMS/BAMS/BCMS Stack; Liquid Cooling Loop; SCADA Uplink to Fab DCS**
Diesel Engine; Alternator; Fuel Day-Tank; Controller/Transfer Switch
PV Module Array; String Inverter / Central Inverter; DC Combiner; Rooftop/Ground-Mount Racking

rows.Competence Required: PV EPC, MV coupling, DSO interface

rows.Rationale: Already delivered as part of BESS EPC scope
PCS hardware overlaps; fab-grade <1 cycle response is new
LFP + PCS know-how transfers; IT-grade N+1 redundancy and tool interfaces are new
Civil + electrical transfer; rotating-machine service is new

rows.Competence Required: HV design, Type A certification, TSO interface
SEMI F47, cleanroom installation, tool FATs

rows.Competence Gap: Fab-grade redundancy (N+1), tool-level form factors, SEMI F47 cert
rows.Rationale: ~50% overlap (battery + PCS); tool-interface engineering is gap

rows.Competence Gap: Sub-cycle response, series injection transformer, IEC 61000-4-34
rows.Rationale: ~40% overlap (power electronics); fab certification path is new
~30% overlap (civil + ATS); engine-gen not core; low margin
Mein Solar resells; does not manufacture switchgear itself

rows.Competence Gap: HV design, VDE-AR-N 4120, Type A cert

rows.What Must Be Added: HV substation capability, dynamic-UPS portfolio, tool-level static UPS, facility-wide ATS, 24/7 mission-critical O&M org, fab PM reference
rows.Competitors at Target: Siemens Smart Infrastructure, ABB Electrification, Schneider Electric Critical Power, Eaton
rows.Economic Rationale: Moving from L5 component to L6 integrator requires a fundamentally different sales motion (fab GC and Facility Director, not IPP), much larger balance sheet for SEMI F47-backed availability guarantees, and 24/7 on-site service capability Mein Solar does not have. TAM at L6 is ~€150–300M per new fab greenfield, but organic capture requires ~€30M+ investment and ≥3 fab references. Acquisition of a fab-power specialist is the only realistic path — and even then competing against Siemens Smart Infrastructure on an Infineon/ESMC bid is asymmetric.

rows.Check: ≥2 L4 per L5 at Mein Solar's level
rows.Status: PASS (4–8 L4 per L5 under L6H-POWER)

rows.Check: Full depth to company level with all siblings enumerated
rows.Status: PASS (all 8 L5 alternatives under L6H-POWER enumerated)

rows.Read: **Tangential** — BESS is not in the wafer fab's value-creation chain; it's in the horizontal Power layer. Fab CFJ does not require BESS; it requires *uninterrupted* power, of which BESS is one of several answers (and today the smallest).

Real but narrow: BESS is displacing flywheel + static UPS hybrids when (a) the fab also wants grid-services revenue stacking, (b) the fab owner has renewable/PPA ambitions co-located. Infineon Dresden, ESMC Dresden, Bosch SiC Reutlingen all fit this shape.
**HIGH**. SEMI F47 / ITIC certification is non-negotiable. First-reference problem is acute: no fab owner takes a BESS-for-ride-through reference risk on an unproven vendor.

Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency is the most transferable asset — "PV+BESS behind-the-meter with grid-services revenue stack" framed as a **decarbonization + Netzentgelte reduction** play is more saleable than "ride-through" alone.

**Consortium / subcontractor** into an existing fab GC (M+W, Jacobs, Exyte, Arcadis) rather than direct-prime. Start with the PV + BESS behind-the-meter scope where Mein Solar's existing certification and PV-EPC reference apply. Ride-through-grade product follows a fab-specific qualification cycle.

rows.Dimension: Priority vs. other markets in the set
rows.Read: **MEDIUM — defer**. Architecture distance 6, adoption *emerging*, no current reference. Utilities (221118/114/115), C&I landlords (531120), HPC hubs (457120), and data centers (518210) have shorter entry paths and higher confidence. Revisit Semiconductor once 2–3 of the above are landed and reference a "mission-critical" installation.

entities.cfj_statement: Fabricate integrated circuits and discrete semiconductor devices on wafers at nanometer-scale precision with yield-acceptable electrical performance, then package and qualify them for sale

secondary_positions: L5: MV Switchgear & Distribution (under L6H-POWER, via integrated BESS scope)

units.name: Semiconductor Device Manufacturing Ecosystem
units.description: Economic community of IDMs, foundries, fabless, OSAT, equipment OEMs, materials suppliers, utilities

units.name: Wafer Preparation
units.description: Prepare clean defect-free substrate; incoming inspection, clean, epi

units.name: Lithography & Patterning
units.description: Transfer circuit pattern onto wafer via photoresist coat, expose, develop

units.name: Etch & Clean
units.description: Remove material per pattern; strip resist; post-etch clean

units.name: Doping & Implantation
units.description: Modify electrical properties of Si lattice via ion implant + anneal

units.name: Deposition & Growth
units.description: Deposit insulating/conductive films (oxidation, CVD, PVD, ALD, epi)

units.name: CMP
units.description: Planarize wafer surface between layers; polish + post-CMP clean

units.name: Metallization & Interconnect
units.description: Build conductive interconnect stack (Cu damascene, Al)

units.name: Wafer Probe & Sort
units.description: Electrically test each die on wafer; yield feedback

units.name: Assembly & Packaging
units.description: Singulate and package die into shippable IC

units.name: Final Test & Qualification
units.description: ATE, burn-in, reliability screen on packaged ICs

units.name: Electrical Power Infrastructure
units.description: Horizontal. Deliver clean, continuous, ride-through MV/LV power to every tool and the cleanroom envelope

units.name: Ultrapure Water & Wastewater
units.description: Horizontal. UPW supply, chemical wastewater, solvent recovery

units.name: Process Gases & Chemicals
units.description: Horizontal. Bulk/specialty gas yards, chemical distribution, abatement

units.name: Cleanroom Environmental Control
units.description: Horizontal. ISO 1–5 HVAC, AMC scrubbing, vibration isolation

units.name: MES & Industrial IT/OT
units.description: Horizontal. Recipe mgmt, equipment integration, SCADA, cybersecurity

units.name: HV/MV Incoming Substation
units.description: 110 kV → MV step-down; utility interface; Type A plant cert

units.name: MV Switchgear & Distribution
units.description: 10–36 kV switchgear, busbar, protection, metering. MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY (as part of BESS scope)

units.name: Static Battery UPS
units.description: Facility/tool-level double-conversion UPS with battery backup

units.name: Dynamic UPS / Flywheel
units.description: Rotating kinetic-energy UPS; sub-cycle ride-through + diesel ride-over

units.name: Active Voltage Conditioner
units.description: DVR / series-injection conditioner for voltage-sag ride-through (SEMI F47)

units.name: BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through
units.description: MEIN SOLAR PRIMARY POSITION. Containerized Li-ion BESS with bidirectional PCS, MV coupling, EMS; ride-through + grid-services revenue stack

units.name: Backup Generator
units.description: Diesel/gas genset for extended outage backup; ATS-switched

units.name: On-Site PV Generation

direct_competence_transfer_04e.rationale: Incumbent PV EPC business; ≥10 yr reference
Already delivered inside BESS EPC scope
PCS hardware overlaps; fab-grade sub-cycle response is new
LFP + PCS know-how transfers; IT-grade N+1 redundancy is new
Civil + electrical transfer; rotating-machine service is new

direct_competence_transfer_excluded: HV/MV Incoming Substation — HV Type A cert gap
Dynamic UPS / Flywheel — rotating-machine design gap
All wafer-process L5 tools (L6a–L6j) — SEMI-specific engineering and references gap

strategic_adjacency_expansion_04f.competence_gap: Fab-grade N+1, tool-level form factors, SEMI F47 cert

strategic_adjacency_expansion_04f.rationale: ~30% overlap; engine-gen not core, low margin

vertical_move_up_04g.what_must_be_added: HV substation capability, dynamic-UPS portfolio, tool-level static UPS, facility-wide ATS, 24/7 mission-critical O&M org, fab PM reference
vertical_move_up_04g.economic_rationale: Organic capture requires ~€30M+ investment and ≥3 fab references; TAM at L6 is ~€150–300M per greenfield fab but acquisition is the only realistic path

strategic_read.entry_mode_recommendation: Consortium / subcontractor into fab GC (M+W, Jacobs, Exyte, Arcadis). Lead with PV+BESS behind-the-meter scope (Netzentgelte + decarbonization), not ride-through. Ride-through follows fab-specific SEMI F47 qualification cycle.
dealkiller_risks: SEMI F47 / ITIC certification not yet demonstrated on Mein Solar BESS product
No fab reference; first-reference risk is unacceptable to fab owners
Mission-critical availability SLA and 24/7 O&M org not in place
Scale mismatch — a single fab's ride-through BESS is ~2–20 MWh, Mein Solar reference is 9 MWh; fits, but fab owner demands fab-sector prior art
strongest_hooks: PV+BESS behind-the-meter for decarbonization (Infineon, ESMC, Bosch all have public Net-Zero targets)
Netzentgelte reduction via peak shaving (measurable CFO outcome)
Single-source EPC simplifies fab facility-engineering vendor list

qa.Q4_l4_min_2_per_l5: PASS at Mein Solar's level (3–4 L4 per L5 under L6H-POWER)
qa.Q7_unit_count_in_range: PASS (~66 units — above soft-target 40 but semiconductor industry justifies, see Q7 soft-fail threshold)
qa.Q9_full_depth_with_all_siblings: PASS (all 8 L5 alternatives under L6H-POWER enumerated with L4 subsystems)
